title: Exploring the potential of simple rainfall-runoff models for estimating urban stormwater runoff with rain gauge and weather radar rainfall.
author_string: Nielsen, Janni Mosekær, Rasmussen, Michael Robdrup, Thorndahl, Søren, Kristensen, Lene Werner, Nielsen, Jesper Ellerbæk
journal_full: Water science and technology : a journal of the International Association on Water Pollution Research
abstract: In urban drainage, hydrodynamically (HD) based models are often used for urban runoff estimation. Such models are computationally and data demanding, limiting their application, especially in real time. On the other hand, rainfall-runoff hydrologic models are rapid models that are more suitable for real-time applications. However, the use of hydrologic models requires knowledge of their performance relative to the performance of HD-based models. This paper demonstrates the potential of three simpler rainfall-runoff models in a small urban catchment. We show that a time-area surface model with a simple drainage routing model, a linear reservoir model, and an artificial neural network model performs with similar accuracy as a HD-based model. It is demonstrated that urban rainfall-runoff is modeled with high accuracy using both rain gauge and weather radar rainfall. With rain gauge rainfall, the artificial neural network performs the best of both simplified and HD-based models, with Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency (NSE) = 0.97 on event-accumulated runoff and NSE = 0.83 on a 5-min time series. Using weather radar, rainfall reduces the runoff prediction accuracy. All investigated rainfall-runoff models are, however, shown to produce reliable runoff predictions in small urban areas using rain gauges and weather radar rainfall.
